lesson.title: CAPS.Mathematics.Gr12.P2.STAT: Symmetric and skewed data. Variance and standard deviatio...
lesson.explanation: Grade 12 statistics covers measures of dispersion and regression. Standard deviation σ = √[Σ(xᵢ − x̄)²/n] measures spread around the mean. For grouped data, use the class midpoints. The regression line ŷ = a + bx (least squares) is the line of best fit for bivariate data. The correlation coefficient r (−1 ≤ r ≤ 1) measures the strength and direction of the linear relationship.

practice_problems.question: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of the data set: 3, 5, 7, 7, 8, 10, 12.
hints: Mean = sum of all values divided by the number of values.
For standard deviation, find each (xᵢ − x̄)², sum them, divide by n, then take the square root.

practice_problems.question: The five-number summary for a data set is: Min = 12, Q1 = 25, Median = 34, Q3 = 48, Max = 61. Draw a box-and-whisker diagram and describe the skewness.
practice_problems.answer: The box spans from 25 to 48 with a median line at 34. Whiskers extend from 12 to 61. The data is approximately symmetrical (median roughly centred in the box).
hints: Mark Q1, median, and Q3 on a number line and draw the box between Q1 and Q3.
Compare the distances: median − Q1 vs Q3 − median to judge skewness.
practice_problems.explanation: Median − Q1 = 34 − 25 = 9. Q3 − Median = 48 − 34 = 14. The right half of the box is slightly wider, suggesting a mild positive skew. The whiskers: left = 25 − 12 = 13, right = 61 − 48 = 13 (equal). Overall shape is close to symmetrical with slight right skew.
distractors: Cannot determine skewness from a box plot

practice_problems.question: A data set has Q1 = 20, Q3 = 44. Determine whether the values 0 and 60 are outliers.
practice_problems.answer: IQR = 24. Lower fence = −16, upper fence = 80. Neither 0 nor 60 is an outlier.
hints: IQR = Q3 − Q1. An outlier lies below Q1 − 1.5×IQR or above Q3 + 1.5×IQR.
Calculate both fences and check each value.
practice_problems.explanation: IQR = 44 − 20 = 24. Lower fence = 20 − 1.5(24) = 20 − 36 = −16. Upper fence = 44 + 1.5(24) = 44 + 36 = 80. Since 0 > −16 and 60 < 80, neither value is an outlier.

practice_problems.question: A scatter plot shows the relationship between hours studied (x) and test score (y). The correlation coefficient is r = 0.87. Interpret this value and state whether a linear model is suitable.
practice_problems.answer: There is a strong positive linear correlation between hours studied and test score. A linear model is suitable because |r| is close to 1.
hints: The sign of r tells direction (positive/negative). The magnitude tells strength.
practice_problems.explanation: r = 0.87 is positive (as x increases, y increases) and close to 1 (strong relationship). Since the linear correlation is strong, a linear regression model is appropriate for predictions within the data range.
distractors: Weak positive correlation; linear model is not suitable
Strong negative correlation; linear model is suitable

practice_problems.question: Given the data: x = {2, 4, 6, 8, 10} and y = {5, 9, 14, 18, 23}, calculate the least squares regression line ŷ = a + bx. (Use: x̄ = 6, ȳ = 13.8, Σ(xᵢ − x̄)(yᵢ − ȳ) = 72, Σ(xᵢ − x̄)² = 40.)
hints: b = Σ(xᵢ − x̄)(yᵢ − ȳ) / Σ(xᵢ − x̄)².

practice_problems.question: Using the regression line ŷ = 3.0 + 1.8x from the previous question (data range x ∈ [2, 10]), predict the y-value when x = 7 and when x = 25. Comment on the reliability of each prediction.
practice_problems.answer: When x = 7: ŷ = 3.0 + 1.8(7) = 15.6 (reliable, within data range). When x = 25: ŷ = 3.0 + 1.8(25) = 48.0 (unreliable, extrapolation far beyond data range).
hints: Substitute each x-value into the equation.
Interpolation (within data range) is reliable; extrapolation (outside) is not.
practice_problems.explanation: x = 7 falls within [2, 10] so the prediction ŷ = 15.6 is an interpolation and is reliable. x = 25 is far outside the data range, so ŷ = 48.0 is an extrapolation and may be very inaccurate because the linear trend may not continue.
distractors: x = 25 is reliable because r is strong

practice_problems.question: Class A scored: 65, 70, 72, 75, 78, 80, 85 (mean = 75, σ ≈ 6.16). Class B scored: 40, 55, 70, 75, 80, 95, 110 (mean = 75, σ ≈ 21.60). Both classes have the same mean. Which class performed more consistently? Justify using at least two statistical measures.
practice_problems.answer: Class A performed more consistently. Both have mean 75, but Class A has σ ≈ 6.16 vs Class B's σ ≈ 21.60. Class A's range is 20 (85 − 65) vs Class B's range of 70 (110 − 40). The smaller standard deviation and range indicate less spread.
hints: Consistency means less spread. Compare standard deviations.
Also compare ranges and consider whether outliers affect your conclusion.
practice_problems.explanation: Equal means (75) make direct comparison meaningful. Class A: σ ≈ 6.16, range = 20, IQR = 80 − 70 = 10. Class B: σ ≈ 21.60, range = 70, IQR = 95 − 55 = 40. All three measures of dispersion confirm Class A is far more consistent.
distractors: Class B is more consistent because it has a wider range
They are equally consistent because the means are equal
Cannot compare because the sample sizes are equal

hints: Remember the empirical rule: 68% within 1σ, 95% within 2σ, 99.7% within 3σ.
Remember the empirical rule: 68% within 1σ, 95% within 2σ, 99.7% within 3σ.
Remember the empirical rule: 68% within 1σ, 95% within 2σ, 99.7% within 3σ.

key_vocabulary.definition: A measure of how spread out data is from the mean
key_vocabulary.example_usage: A small σ means data is clustered near the mean.

key_vocabulary.definition: The square of the standard deviation, σ²

key_vocabulary.example_usage: r = 0.95 indicates a strong positive correlation.

key_vocabulary.definition: The line of best fit ŷ = a + bx
key_vocabulary.example_usage: Use the regression equation to predict values.

misconceptions.misconception: Students divide by (n−1) instead of n when calculating population standard deviation.
misconceptions.correction: In Matric, we use σ = √[Σ(x−x̄)²/n] (divide by n). The (n−1) version is for sample standard deviation, which is not in the CAPS syllabus.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: Textbooks and calculators may use both. In the exam, always divide by n.

assessment.title: Assessment: Symmetric and skewed data. Variance and standard deviation (σ = √(Σ(x−x̄)²/n)). Identify outliers. Ogives, box-and-whisker plots, histograms, frequency polygons. Normal distribution (intuitive). Bivariate data: scatter plots, least-squares regression line ŷ = a + bx, correlation coefficient r

items.question: Given r = −0.92 for a dataset, describe the correlation and state whether a linear model is appropriate.
items.correct_answer: Strong negative correlation. A linear model is appropriate because |r| is close to 1.

flashcards.front: What is the formula for standard deviation?
flashcards.back: σ = √[Σ(xᵢ − x̄)²/n] where x̄ is the mean and n is the number of data values.
